segments.text: Jane: Hello, North Bay Aquatic Centre, Jane speaking.
Peter: Oh, hello.
Peter: I'm calling to apply for a part-time lifeguard position.
Peter: Am I speaking to the right person?
Jane: Yes, you are.
Jane: We are currently recruiting lifeguards for two different positions.
Jane: But first, I'll have to jot down some of your personal information before we officially invite you for an interview.
Jane: Is that alright?
Peter: Sure.
Peter: No problem.
Jane: May I have your name, please?
Peter: It's Peter, Peter Smith.
Jane: Okay.
Jane: And what is your current address?
Peter: It's 130 South Main Street, Lake Elsinore.
Jane: I'm sorry, Elsinore?
Peter: Oh, it's E-L-S-I-N-O-R-E, Elsinore.
Jane: Right.
Jane: Got it!
Jane: What is your phone number?
Peter: My home phone number is 0-double 7-double 9-5-3-6-2.
Peter: Actually, it's easier to reach me by my cellphone number.
Peter: It's 0-double 7-8-9-6-2-4-5.
Peter: I'm mainly out these days.
Jane: Right.
Jane: Can you tell me about your work experience?
Peter: Well, I now work as a waiter.
Peter: And that's just a part-time gig.
Peter: It requires serving customers, and I also have some cleaning duties.
Jane: Okay.
Jane: Do you have any other part-time job?
Peter: No.
Peter: But I'm majoring in physical education right now.
Peter: I'm learning exercise physiology, sports nutrition and individual fitness activities.
Peter: And I want to become a baseball coach for a local high school.
Jane: So, do you have any other relevant work experience to a lifeguard position?
Peter: Oh, right.
Peter: I also worked part-time as a rescue diver for a local beach last summer.
Peter: I'm certified for open water rescue missions.
Jane: Excellent.
Jane: That's exactly what we're looking for.
Jane: Before we continue, I need to know a few more things to ensure that you are qualified for maintaining water safety during work.
Peter: Okay.
Peter: Go ahead.
Jane: As a lifeguard, you will spend most of your time monitoring the area near the water and reducing risks to everyone here.
Jane: So we are looking for someone who can stay focused.
Jane: Do you have difficulties concentrating or any related disorders?
Peter: No.
Peter: I don't have any medical disorders relating to that sort of problem.
Jane: We're also expecting someone with good eyesight because a lifeguard must be able to recognise and respond to any struggling swimmers quickly.
Peter: I have sharp vision.
Peter: I mean, both eyes are 20-10 without correction.
Jane: Good.
Jane: Do you have relevant skills in terms of lifeguarding?
Peter: I'm good at diving.
Peter: You know my previous job requires training in that.
Jane: We only need lifeguards with certification.
Jane: I assume you already have one, right?
Peter: Yes.
Peter: To get the certificate, I received training for waterfront lifeguarding two years ago.
Peter: The training includes things like providing first aid and emergency care.
Peter: But mine is about to expire in November.
Peter: Oh, wait, let me check.
Peter: It's a month earlier, in October.
Jane: Okay.
Jane: You're good for now before it expires.
Jane: But if you want to work here for the whole year, you'll have to renew it.
Peter: I see.
Jane: What time do you prefer to work?
Peter: I already work part-time in a restaurant on weekdays, so I'd like to work on the weekend.
Jane: We already have enough people working here on Sundays.
Peter: I can work on Saturday mornings then.
Jane: Right.
Peter: And I can start from 6 o'clock in the morning.
Peter: I'm a morning person.
Jane: Sounds great.
Jane: Where did you hear about our recruitment information from?
Peter: I heard it on radio this morning while driving.
Peter: I wouldn't say like reading newspapers.
Jane: Thank you for calling.
Jane: We'll notify you about the interview details in two days.
Peter: Thank you.
Peter: Bye.
Jane: Goodbye.
